div.p: num_active > 0 loop — decodes every stop before resuming
WIFEXITED path fprintf: "[pid N] +++ exited with %d +++" pid_map_remove(&g_pid_map, pid) num_active-- continue → next waitpid
WIFSIGNALED path fprintf: "[pid N] +++ killed by signal N (name) +++" pid_map_remove(&g_pid_map, pid) num_active-- continue → next waitpid

handle_ptrace_event(pid, event, map, &num_active) FORK/VFORK/CLONE: PTRACE_GETEVENTMSG → new_pid pid_map_get(new_pid) → fresh state num_active++ PTRACE_SETOPTIONS(new_pid, TRACE_OPTIONS) PTRACE_SYSCALL(new_pid, 0, 0) ← resume child EXEC: state→in_syscall = 1; outbuf_len = 0 memset(current); log completion EXIT: PTRACE_GETEVENTMSG → exit_status preview (do NOT pid_map_remove — process still alive)

Syscall entry/exit stop state = pid_map_get(&g_pid_map, pid) state→in_syscall = !state→in_syscall ← toggle handle_syscall_stop(pid, state) Entry (in_syscall==1): extract_args; clock_gettime(entry_time) build outbuf: "[pid N] name(args" Exit (in_syscall==0): clock_gettime(exit_time); elapsed_ns stats_record(nr, retval, elapsed_ns) ← always if !filter_matches(nr): discard, return append ") = retval\n"; fputs(outbuf, g_output)
Plain SIGTRAP (exec initial stop, breakpoint, or post-attach stop — not a syscall stop) Do NOT flip in_syscall toggle
SIGSTOP stop Likely: initial stop of auto-attached child (synthetic — no real signal to deliver) Swallow: do NOT re-inject SIGSTOP (avoids freezing child waiting for SIGCONT)
Signal-delivery stop (SIGTERM, SIGUSR1, …) Re-inject: pass sig to PTRACE_SYSCALL ⚠ Passing 0 would SUPPRESS the signal

g.text: check at top of each iteration
